policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-009, CS-RULE-011, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-021, FIN-RULE-003]
expected_outcome: Agent resolves at his seat — modem power-cycle restored speed. No back-office ticket required.

coverage.notes: mobile vs home, all devices vs one, sudden vs gradual
speed test → device count → 5GHz → modem reboot

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Marcus. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hi, my name is Sofia Reyes. I'm having problems with my internet — it's very, very slow.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Hi, Sofia, I'm sorry to hear that. I understand you're calling about slow internet, and I can definitely help you with that. First, I just need to verify a few things on the account. Could you confirm your full account number for me?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes. It is seven-seven-nine-zero, three-three-two-two, one-five.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Got it. Could you confirm the last four digits of the payment method we have on file?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Um, I think it's the credit card? Last four is two-six-five-five.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Perfect, that matches. And one more — could you tell me the last four digits of your social security or national ID, or the security PIN, whichever is easier?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: The security PIN is one-zero-zero-eight.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Wonderful. Sofia, you're fully verified. Thanks for being patient with that.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No problem.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: So I want to acknowledge — slow internet is honestly one of the most frustrating things, especially when you're trying to actually get something done. Let me ask a few quick questions so we can figure out exactly what's happening, okay?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes, please.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: First — is this your home internet, or are you having mobile data issues?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Home internet. The WiFi.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Got it. Is the slow speed affecting all of your devices, or just one?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: All of them, I think. My laptop, my phone, the smart TV. Everything is slow.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay, that's helpful. And was it sudden — like one day it just changed — or has it been getting slower over time?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: It started maybe four days ago. Suddenly. Before that it was fine.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Perfect. Thank you. Has anything changed in the home recently — new device, a router move, a power outage?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hmm. There was a small power flicker last week. The lights blinked. Maybe Wednesday?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's a useful clue. Let me first check that there's no outage in your area. One moment.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay — your account is active, no billing holds, and there are no reported outages in your service area. So this looks like a local issue we can troubleshoot together. Are you near your modem and router right now?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes, I am sitting next to it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Wonderful. The first thing I'd like to do is run a speed test, but I need it to be accurate — so I need you to plug your laptop directly into the modem with an ethernet cable, if you have one. WiFi tests aren't precise enough for this.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Ethernet cable… one moment, let me check… yes, I have one. Hold on, I will plug it in.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Take your time, Sofia.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay… plugging in now…

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay, it is plugged in.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Great. Now please open a browser and go to nexalink dot speedtest. When the page loads, click the big start button and read me the download number when it finishes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay… loading… clicking start now…

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm still here with you, Sofia — just waiting for the result. Thank you for your patience.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: It says… download is twenty-eight Mbps.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And which plan are you on — do you remember? Looking here, I see you're on the two hundred Mbps fiber plan.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Two hundred? But I'm getting twenty-eight? That's like ten percent.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's a real problem. You should be seeing close to two hundred on a wired test. Let me have you do a couple of things. How many devices are connected to your WiFi right now? Anything downloading or backing up?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Maybe… seven devices? But nothing is downloading. The TV is off.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay. Seven is normal — that's not the cause. The fact that the wired speed is this low points to the modem itself. I'd like to power-cycle it. That means unplugging the power cable from the back of the modem, waiting a full thirty seconds, and plugging it back in. Can you do that?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes. Unplugging now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thirty seconds. I'll count with you if you'd like, or you can just plug it back in when you're ready. The lights on the front will start blinking — that's normal. Wait until they go solid before we test again. That usually takes one to two minutes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay… plugged in… the lights are blinking now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Perfect. Let's give it a moment. While we wait — that power flicker you mentioned last Wednesday is most likely what caused this. The modem can hang in a degraded state after a brief power loss. The full power-cycle is what fixes it.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Oh, interesting. So a small flicker can do this?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Surprisingly, yes — the modem doesn't always recover automatically. Are the lights solid yet?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes, they are solid now. Green and white.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Wonderful. Now let's run the speed test one more time on the wired connection.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay… running it…

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Oh! Two hundred and twelve Mbps! It's working!

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: There we go! That's right where you should be. So just to summarize what happened — the brief power flicker last week left the modem in a degraded state, the full thirty-second power cycle reset its connection to the network, and you're now getting full speed. Does that all make sense?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes, it makes sense. Thank you so much, Marcus. I was thinking I would have to buy a new router.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: No, your equipment is fine. And just so you know for the future — if you ever see speeds drop suddenly again, the very first thing to try is that thirty-second power cycle. If it ever doesn't fix it, give us a call right away. Is there anything else I can help you with today, Sofia?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, that is all. You were very helpful.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for saying that. Your case reference number for today is N-X-L dash four-four-eight-zero-three-six-six. After we hang up, you'll get a quick survey — if you have a moment, your feedback really helps us.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Of course. Goodbye, Marcus.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Have a wonderful day, Sofia.
